text.title: Solo Content Calendar — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every weekly content row must enforce. These are HARD rules — inspiration or novelty cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each row MUST cite a `pain_ref` from the ICP pain ladder; rows pinned to a pain that no longer ranks in the top-5 are auto-flagged for retirement at quarterly review.

rationale.source: ICP-fit research (April Dunford Obviously Awesome, JTBD discipline)

rule.rationale: Content unpinned from ranked ICP pain drifts to entertainment; for solo founders entertainment never converts.

rule.statement: Cadence MUST be exactly one shippable asset per week_iso; two assets in one week and zero the next is a violation, not a balance.

rationale.source: SEO + audience-conditioning research on cadence consistency

rule.rationale: Search and audience algorithms reward regularity, not bursts; the rule encodes that explicitly.

rule.statement: Next week's row MUST be at status `drafted` by Friday EOD of the current week; failure replaces the next week's writing block with a backfill block on a stalled row.
rule.rationale: Blank-page anxiety on Monday kills the week; carrying a draft into the writing block keeps cadence under stress.

rule.statement: Each row MUST declare ONE distribution channel and ONE primary keyword OR hook (not both, not three); the row owns one bet per asset.

rationale.source: Distribution-first content discipline (Justin Welsh / Amanda Natividad analyses)

rule.rationale: Multi-channel asset spread thins the launch energy; one channel + one bet outperforms split campaigns at solo scale.

rule.statement: Quarterly review MUST retire assets whose pain_ref left the top-5 OR whose publish-to-conversion ratio is below the calendar's median for ≥ 8 weeks; retirement = stop promoting, do not delete.

rationale.source: Content-pruning research (Detailed.com / Aleyda Solís content audits)

rule.rationale: Old underperformers eat crawl budget and audience trust; quarterly pruning lifts the rest of the catalog.

example.title: Anchored content row
example.code: week_iso: 2026-W22 pain_ref: P2 # "founders cannot tell which features paid users actually use" asset_type: blog_post title: "5 SQL queries every solo SaaS founder runs every Monday" target_keyword: "solo saas monday metrics queries" distribution_channel: seo publish_date: 2026-05-28 status: drafted
example.note: Pinned to a ranked pain, one channel, one keyword bet, drafted by Friday.

example.title: Un-anchored content row
example.code: "Maybe write something about AI agents this week? Or that funny story from yesterday?"
example.note: No pain ref, no channel, no draft, no status. The row will not ship and is a leading indicator of the calendar's death.
